title: Resume Analyzer: NLP Preprocessing, Semantic Similarity, and Prediction Explanations

requirements.text: Enhance the backend ATS score computation to include NLP preprocessing steps — stopword removal and lemmatization — on the resume text before performing TF-IDF cosine similarity comparison against the job description for the selected role. The preprocessed text should be used for all downstream scoring.

quotes: ATS Feature: NLP preprocessing with stopword removal and lemmatization before similarity comparison

acceptanceCriteria: Resume text undergoes stopword removal and lemmatization before ATS similarity scoring
The ATS score returned still falls in the 0–100 range
Preprocessing is applied consistently to both resume text and job description text prior to comparison

requirements.text: Enhance the Job Role Prediction backend logic to return, alongside the top 3 predicted roles and confidence percentages, an explanation for each prediction. The explanation must list the skills detected in the resume and the matching factors (keywords/skills) that contributed to that role's confidence score.

quotes: Provide explanation for prediction (skills detected + matching factors)
Job Role Prediction: Provide explanation for prediction including detected skills and matching factors

acceptanceCriteria: Each of the top 3 predicted roles includes a list of detected skills from the resume
Each prediction includes a list of matching factors (skills/keywords) that drove the confidence score
The explanation data is returned as part of the existing prediction response structure

requirements.text: Update the Job Role Prediction frontend section to display the explanation for each of the top 3 predicted roles. For each role, show the list of detected skills and the matching factors beneath or alongside the existing confidence bar/chart visualization.

quotes: Provide explanation for prediction (skills detected + matching factors)

acceptanceCriteria: Each of the top 3 predicted job role cards/rows displays the detected skills list
Each card/row also shows the matching factors that contributed to the prediction
The explanation is presented clearly without cluttering the confidence visualization

constraints: All backend logic must remain in a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo — do not introduce Python, spaCy, or Sentence Transformers as they are not supported on this platform
NLP preprocessing (stopword removal, lemmatization) must be implemented in Motoko or client-side TypeScript using available patterns in frontend/src/lib/resumeAnalysis.ts
Do not modify immutable frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui
Frontend must use React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and React Query — no Streamlit or Python frontend
nonGoals: Introducing Python, FastAPI, spaCy, Sentence Transformers, or any external ML library backend
Adding a Streamlit frontend or any non-React UI framework
Implementing Logistic Regression or Naive Bayes ML models (not supported on this platform)
Adding SQLite or any external database
Creating a new modular folder structure — the existing project structure is retained
Re-implementing features already live: resume upload, ATS scoring, skill gap analysis, career recommendations, resume improvement suggestions, dashboard UI
